channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: GM Wants Your Electric Car to Power Your House—and Your Neighborhood
item.description: The automaker today is turning on vehicle-to-grid charging for its GM Energy customers. Will people actually use it?

item.title: GM thinks EVs can help offset AI’s energy suck with vehicle-to-grid tech
item.description: At an event in San Francisco today, General Motors made a series of announcements around EV batteries, energy storage, and grid resiliency in the face of growing electricity demand from AI data centers. The automaker announced that it would be activating new vehicle-to-grid capabilities for its current EV and home energy customers. It's releasing a […]...

item.title: GM joins race to build batteries for AI data centers and the grid
item.description: GM is developing an entirely new sodium-ion battery chemistry for use in everything from data centers to its own factories.

item.title: Hey Siri, here’s what I actually want from AI
item.description: I'm desperate for a personal AI assistant, but do I really want to become the kind of person who can't function without the friendly robot voice in my phone?

item.title: The hidden emotional cost of leadership
item.description: Justin McLeod, the founder of Hinge and CEO at Overtone is a modern tech leader who openly acknowledges the emotional weight of building a company centered on human relationships. This is his truth about leadership. Justin McLeod, the founder of Hinge and CEO at Overtone is a modern tech leader who openly acknowledges the emotional weight of building a company centered on human relationships. This is his truth about leadership....

item.title: Anthropic’s Fable 5 can make weirdly fun video games with the click of a button
item.description: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 is going to be a big hit with the web's vibe coders.

item.title: This health startup will create a weekly podcast just for you—starring your bloodwork
item.description: Eternal, founded by the Athletic’s Alex Mather, aims to turn people’s health data into AI-generated media that’s easier to digest than a lab report. We’re inundated with data, but for many people, finding a way to make sense of it is elusive.

item.title: Microsoft AI head calls out Anthropic for acting like Claude is conscious
item.description: Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman says it's "really, really dangerous" for Anthropic to speculate about Claude's consciousness inside its "constitution," or the instructions that tell the model how to behave. During an episode of Decoder, Suleyman argues that this kind of speculation may have set up the chatbot to act as though it's conscious: I […]...

item.title: Anthropic's Fable AI brings the capabilities of its unreleased Mythos model to regular users
item.description: Claude subscribers can try the model until June 22 without spending usage credits.

item.title: « Test cedric » : la bourde d’un développeur paralyse l’application du Crédit Agricole
item.description: Ce 9 juin 2026, des clients du Crédit Agricole ont reçu une notification « Test cedric » sur l'appli Ma Banque, suivie de pannes de connexion. Pas de piratage en vue : tout pointe vers un test technique parti en vrille, même si le Crédit Agricole n'a pas encore confirmé l'origine de l'incident.

item.title: Le chargeur idéal pour votre table de chevet est en promotion chez Amazon
item.description: Marre de multiplier les câbles sur votre table de chevet ? Le chargeur compact Ugreen MagFlow 2-en-1 (qui permet de charger de front votre iPhone et vos AirPods en induction) s'affiche actuellement à 26,98 euros au lieu de 35,99 euros sur Amazon.

item.title: Huawei just dropped a major AI chip surprise — but Nvidia should really be paying attention to the other big news from its rival
item.description: The Ascend 950DT announcement mattered less than Huawei’s rapid development cadence and increasingly embedded artificial intelligence infrastructure ecosystem.

item.title: Anthropic lance Fable 5, son IA « Mythos » la plus puissante, mais bridée volontairement
item.description: Anthropic ouvre aujourd'hui au grand public le modèle le plus puissant qu'il ait jamais rendu accessible, Claude Fable 5. Il est gros, et ce monstre de calcul refuse de répondre sur certains sujets et bascule alors vers une IA moins douée.

item.title: On a trouvé le ventilateur de poche le plus brutal (et le moins cher) du moment
item.description: Le ventilateur de poche Fiizreal X50 ne fait pas dans la dentelle avec sa puissance monstrueuse de 240W. On le retrouve actuellement sur Aliexpress à seulement 16,79 euros grâce à un code promo particulièrement avantageux.

item.title: Monteur de PC ou bricoleur ? Cet indispensable kit de tournevis de précision Xiaomi voit son prix baisser
item.description: Amazon propose le kit tournevis de précision de la marque Xiaomi à 16,99 euros au lieu de 19,99 euros. C'est l'accessoire indispensable pour tous les bricoleurs de la tech, monteurs de PC et autres amateurs de démontage méticuleux.

item.title: This Starbucks competitor is the fastest-growing brand in America, says Yelp
item.description: New data suggests coffee and chicken are some of the hottest retail brands right now. When you think of America’s biggest brands, tech companies like Apple, Google, and Amazon; retailers like Walmart and Target, and food and beverage giants like McDonald’s and Starbucks usually come to mind.

item.title: Your sunscreen is outdated. The FDA has finally cleared a path to make it more effective
item.description: Sun protection products in the United States have long lagged behind their counterparts in Europe and Asia. That could change soon. A sunscreen ingredient that has been used across Europe for decades can now officially come stateside.

item.title: Siri AI n’arrive pas en France ? Voici comment le tester quand même sur iOS 27
item.description: Apple a sorti Siri AI, mais pas en Europe. Avec quelques manips et un compte App Store américain, on peut malgré tout rejoindre la liste d’attente depuis la France.

item.title: A single character could be enough to let hackers crack your Linux kernel
item.description: A logic inversion bug was recently found in Linux, caused by a single stray character.

item.title: Orange (et Free) coupent la 2G dans 7 départements du sud-ouest aujourd’hui
item.description: Orange (et donc Free) coupe sa 2G dans sept nouveaux départements du sud-ouest ce 9 juin. Si votre téléphone n’affiche que « E » ou « G » près des barres réseau, il est temps d’agir.

item.title: A hidden summer threat could soon send twice as many Americans to the hospital
item.description: New projections show a dramatic rise in heat-related emergencies across the United States. We know the planet is getting hotter, but some of the grim details about what exactly that means for humanity remain a mystery.

item.title: I spent a month with the ModRetro M64 — and I think there’s no better way to play your classic Nintendo 64 games
item.description: I've been hands-on with the upcoming ModRetro M64 for the past month and I've come away incredibly impressed with the console's ability to play classic Nintendo 64 games with a suite of excellent modern touches.

item.title: EuroOffice arrive pour détrôner Microsoft en Europe, et LibreOffice l’accuse déjà de faire le jeu des américains
item.description: EuroOffice se lance ce 9 juin comme l'alternative européenne à Microsoft et Google. Problème : la fondation derrière LibreOffice l'accuse déjà de faire le jeu de Microsoft.
